steps.title: Human whole blood collected by venipuncture...
steps.instruction: Human whole blood collected by venipuncture and preserved in sodium heparin (green top) tubes (NIH Clinical Center Blood Bank)

steps.title: Heat-inactivated fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Thermo...
steps.instruction: Heat-inactivated fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Thermo Fisher Scientific, catalog number: A

steps.title: Stimulation media (cRPMI) Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Stimulation media (cRPMI) Reagent Final concentration Volume RPMI 1640 + GlutaMax 89% 445 mL Heat-inactivated FBS 10% 50 mL Pen/strep 1% 5 mL Total 500 mL Filter-sterilize the stimulation media through a 500 mL 0.22 μm filtration unit. Stimulation media can be prepared up to one month before use and stored at 4 °C.

steps.title: 1× Thaw/lyse buffer Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 1× Thaw/lyse buffer Reagent Final concentration Volume Molecular biology–grade water 49.95 mL Thaw/lyse concentrate (1,000×) 1× 0.05 mL Total 50.00 mL Filter-sterilize the 1× thaw/lyse buffer through a 500 mL 0.22 μm filtration unit. 1× thaw/lyse buffer can be prepared and stored at room temperature (RT) for up to one month from the preparation date.

steps.title: 1× Barcoding (BC) buffer Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 1× Barcoding (BC) buffer Reagent Final concentration Volume MaxPar PBS 45mL Maxpar ® 10× barcode perm concentrate 1× 5 mL Total 50 mL 1× barcoding buffer can be stored at 4 °C and used for up to one month from the preparation date.

steps.title: Iridium intercalation solution Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Iridium intercalation solution Reagent Final concentration Volume Maxpar ® fix and perm buffer 3.998 mL Cell-ID TM Intercalator-Ir, 12.5 μM 2,000× 0.002 mL Total 4.0 mL Safe handling of iridium requires strict adherence to lab safety protocols for heavy metals. Specific iridium compounds are toxic, corrosive, or irritating. Wear appropriate personal protective equipment and work in a fume hood when applicable. Cell-ID TM intercalator-Ir is light-sensitive. Ensure that any aliquots or solutions are protected from light. Cell-ID TM intercalator-Ir should be aliquoted and frozen at -20 °C immediately after thawing. After a second thaw, aliquots of Cell-ID TM intercalator-Ir should be discarded. The iridium intercalation solution should be prepared immediately before use and stored on wet ice.

steps.title: 1:10 EQ4 beads Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 1:10 EQ4 beads Reagent Final concentration Volume Maxpar ® Cell acquisition solution plus (CAS + ) for CyTOF XT 9 mL EQ Four Element calibration beads 10× 1 mL Total 10 mL The EQ Four Element calibration beads require vigorous shaking by hand for at least 30 s prior to use. Do not vortex the EQ4 beads, as vortexing fails to resuspend the beads into solution. The 1:10 EQ4 beads in CAS + should be prepared immediately before use and stored on ice. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Pipette tips RT LTS 300 μL 768A/8 (Rainin, catalog number:
steps.instruction: Pipette tips RT LTS 300 μL 768A/8 (Rainin, catalog number:

steps.title: Barcodes for whole blood samples can...
steps.instruction: Barcodes for whole blood samples can be prepared the day before processing.

steps.title: A single barcode ampule is sufficient...
steps.instruction: A single barcode ampule is sufficient for two wells of whole blood. For example, two ampules of each barcode BC1-15 were thawed to barcode four whole blood samples.

steps.title: Remove the appropriate barcode ampules from...
steps.instruction: Remove the appropriate barcode ampules from -20 °C and thaw on wet ice.

steps.title: Following the plate layout (see Table...
steps.instruction: Following the plate layout (see Table 3 ), remove enough ampules to provide 50 μL of barcodes per well.

steps.title: Resuspend each ampule of barcodes (BC 1-
steps.instruction: Resuspend each ampule of barcodes (BC 1-

steps.title: Transfer the contents of each barcode...
steps.instruction: Transfer the contents of each barcode ampule into a single well of a 96-well U-bottom plate.

steps.title: Do not exceed two ampules of...
steps.instruction: Do not exceed two ampules of the same barcode in any one well.

steps.title: Store the 96-well U-bottom plate with...
steps.instruction: Store the 96-well U-bottom plate with the resuspended barcodes at 4 °C until ready for use. B. Preparation of stimulation stocks and matrix tubes (1–2 h) Notes:

steps.title: Each matrix tube cap color is...
steps.instruction: Each matrix tube cap color is assigned to a particular stimulation (stimuli).

steps.title: Refer to Table 2 for an...
steps.instruction: Refer to Table 2 for an example of how different stimuli are color-coded for banking and storage at -80 °C.

steps.title: Label 0
steps.instruction: Label 0.5 mL color-coded matrix tubes in accordance with the number of samples and replicates.

steps.title: Prepare all stimulation conditions at 5×...
steps.instruction: Prepare all stimulation conditions at 5× concentrations in stimulation media using sterile 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes. Ensure there is enough volume to aliquot 50 μL into each matrix tube. Refer to the example in Tables 1 and 3 for four whole blood donors, with six replicates of each stimulation condition.

steps.title: For the HIMC stimulation, combine 5×...
steps.instruction: For the HIMC stimulation, combine 5× anti-human CD3 with 5× Protein-A.

steps.title: Aliquot 50 μL of each 5×...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 50 μL of each 5× stimulation stock to the colored matrix tube representative of the stimulation condition banked for that colored tube. For example, 50 μL of 5× IFNα stock is aliquoted into blue-capped matrix tubes.

steps.title: Example stimulation preparation Stimulation [Stock] Stimulant...
steps.instruction: Example stimulation preparation Stimulation [Stock] Stimulant [1×] Stimulant [5×] 5× stimulant (μL) cRPMI (μL) Total volume (μL) UNS Stimulation media NA NA NA NA 1,500.000 IFNα 2.09E+06 IU/mL 10,000 IU/mL 50,000 IU/mL 34.988 1,427.512 1,462.500 LPS 500 μg/mL 1 μg/mL 5 μg/mL 15.000 1,485.000 1,500.000 Anti-human CD3 1,000 μg/mL 10 μg/mL 50 μg/mL 75.000 1,350.000 1,425.000 Protein-A 1,000 μg/mL 10 μg/mL 50 μg/mL 75.000 1,425.000 1,500.000 StemCell activator 50 μL/test 50.000 NEAT NEAT The HIMC stock solution is the combination of anti-human CD3 (cl. UCHT

steps.title: and Protein-A, which is prepared in...
steps.instruction: and Protein-A, which is prepared in a volume equating to 1,500 μL. To prepare the HIMC stock solution, aliquot 75 μL of anti-human CD3 (cl. UCHT

steps.title: and 75 μL of Protein-A into 1,350 μL of cRPMI
steps.instruction: and 75 μL of Protein-A into 1,350 μL of cRPMI. Table

steps.title: Example matrix tube color coding by...
steps.instruction: Example matrix tube color coding by stimulation Matrix tube color Associated (5×) stimulation stock solution Clear Unstimulated (UNS) Yellow Anti-human CD3 (cl. UCHT1) + Protein-A (HIMC) Blue IFNα (IFA) Red StemCell activator (SC) Pink Lipopolysaccharide (LPS) C. Whole blood stimulation and cryopreservation (~1.5 h) Table

steps.title: Example plate layout for whole blood...
steps.instruction: Example plate layout for whole blood lysis and barcoding UNS WB1 Rep1 (BC

steps.title: Whole blood is transferred and lysed...
steps.instruction: Whole blood is transferred and lysed in a 2 mL deep-well plate. The lysed blood samples are transferred from the 2 mL deep-well plate to a sterile 96-well V-bottom plate in the same order as above for barcoding ( Section E ).

steps.title: Prepare a detailed plate layout for...
steps.instruction: Prepare a detailed plate layout for whole blood lysis and barcoding (refer to Table 3 ).

steps.title: Carefully collect whole blood from the designated donor(s) using venipuncture
steps.instruction: Carefully collect whole blood from the designated donor(s) using venipuncture.

steps.title: Immediately transfer the collected blood into...
steps.instruction: Immediately transfer the collected blood into sodium heparin (green top) tubes to prevent clotting.

steps.title: Ensure that blood collection is performed...
steps.instruction: Ensure that blood collection is performed under aseptic conditions to maintain sample integrity.

steps.title: Place the sodium heparin tubes containing...
steps.instruction: Place the sodium heparin tubes containing the whole blood into a 37 °C incubator with 5% CO 2 .

steps.title: Allow the tubes to equilibrate in...
steps.instruction: Allow the tubes to equilibrate in the 37 °C incubator with 5% CO 2 for 30 min to 1 h.

steps.title: Remove the matrix tubes containing the...
steps.instruction: Remove the matrix tubes containing the stock stimulation solutions from -80 °C 20 min before the stimulation cocktail is intended for use.

steps.title: Thaw the matrix tubes in a...
steps.instruction: Thaw the matrix tubes in a 37 °C 5% CO 2 incubator or in a water bath at 37 °C.

steps.title: For each donor sample, ensure that...
steps.instruction: For each donor sample, ensure that one matrix tube of each stimulation stock solution is thawed for every replicate that will be processed.

steps.title: Working in a laminar flow hood,...
steps.instruction: Working in a laminar flow hood, aliquot 200 μL of the whole blood from each tube into the corresponding matrix tubes, adhering to the pre-designed plate layout.

steps.title: Mix the matrix tube contents thoroughly...
steps.instruction: Mix the matrix tube contents thoroughly by pipetting up and down 10×.

steps.title: Work quickly and change pipette tips...
steps.instruction: Work quickly and change pipette tips between each transfer to maintain sample purity and prevent cross-contamination.

steps.title: Tap matrix tubes gently to bring...
steps.instruction: Tap matrix tubes gently to bring the contents of each tube to the bottom.

steps.title: Incubate the matrix tubes in their...
steps.instruction: Incubate the matrix tubes in their sealed holder for 15 min at 37 °C in the 5% CO 2 incubator.

steps.title: Add 250 μL of Prot1 to...
steps.instruction: Add 250 μL of Prot1 to each matrix tube in the same order as the whole blood was added.

steps.title: Using a P300 multichannel pipette, mix...
steps.instruction: Using a P300 multichannel pipette, mix each tube 10×, changing the tips between each tube to prevent contamination.

steps.title: Incubate the matrix tubes at RT for 10 min
steps.instruction: Incubate the matrix tubes at RT for 10 min. Pause point: Stimulated and fixed whole blood can be frozen at -80 °C until it is ready for thawing, processing, and CyTOF analysis. D. Whole blood lysis (2 h) Notes:

steps.title: Immediately proceed to step D2 if...
steps.instruction: Immediately proceed to step D2 if samples were not frozen after stimulation.

steps.title: The matrix tubes containing stimulated and...
steps.instruction: The matrix tubes containing stimulated and fixed whole blood should only be thawed for lysis if all samples can be run on the CyTOF within 1 day (maximum of 60 matrix tubes per run).

steps.title: Thaw the matrix tubes to a...
steps.instruction: Thaw the matrix tubes to a 25 °C water bath for 15 min.

steps.title: Transfer 500 μL of the stimulated...
steps.instruction: Transfer 500 μL of the stimulated and fixed whole blood from each matrix tube into a 2 mL deep-well plate containing 1.2 mL of 1× thaw/lyse buffer.

steps.title: Mix thoroughly using a P1200 multichannel pipette
steps.instruction: Mix thoroughly using a P1200 multichannel pipette.

steps.title: Seal the deep-well plate using a plate sealer
steps.instruction: Seal the deep-well plate using a plate sealer.

steps.title: Allow the whole blood samples to...
steps.instruction: Allow the whole blood samples to lyse for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Centrifuge the deep well containing the...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the deep well containing the lysed whole blood at 548× g for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant with a...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant with a 12-well multichannel, changing tips between each well.

steps.title: Seal the top of the deep-well...
steps.instruction: Seal the top of the deep-well plate using a plate sealer.

steps.title: Gently vortex the sealed plate for...
steps.instruction: Gently vortex the sealed plate for 30 s or until the cell pellets are dispersed.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.6 mL of 1× thaw/lyse buffer and mix thoroughly using a P1200 multichannel pipette.

steps.title: Allow the whole blood sample to...
steps.instruction: Allow the whole blood sample to lyse for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Centrifuge the deep well containing the...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the deep well containing the lysed whole blood at 548× g for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant with a...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant with a 12-well multichannel, changing tips between each well.

steps.title: Seal the top of the deep-well...
steps.instruction: Seal the top of the deep-well plate using a plate sealer and gently vortex the pellet.

steps.title: If not, wash the pellet with 1
steps.instruction: If not, wash the pellet with 1.6 mL of cell staining buffer (CSB). Note: Do not exceed three lysis steps.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells at 974× g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells at 974× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant with a...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant with a 12-well multichannel, changing tips between each well.

steps.title: Leave 50 μL at the bottom of each well
steps.instruction: Leave 50 μL at the bottom of each well.

steps.title: Resuspend each well with 100 μL of CSB
steps.instruction: Resuspend each well with 100 μL of CSB.

steps.title: Transfer 150 μL of cell suspension...
steps.instruction: Transfer 150 μL of cell suspension into a 96-well V-bottom plate and proceed to the barcoding or staining step. E. Barcoding (optional) (1.5 h)

steps.title: Centrifuge the 96-well V-bottom plate containing...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the 96-well V-bottom plate containing the cell suspensions at 974× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Flick the supernatant and blot the...
steps.instruction: Flick the supernatant and blot the plate on a clean paper towel.

steps.title: If any liquid remains in the...
steps.instruction: If any liquid remains in the deep-well plate from step D20, transfer the remaining volume into the 96-well V-bottom plate in the same order as initially added.

steps.title: Centrifuge the 96-well V-bottom plate at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the 96-well V-bottom plate at 974× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Flick the supernatant from the plate...
steps.instruction: Flick the supernatant from the plate and blot on a clean paper towel to remove residual liquid.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 200 μL of...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 200 μL of ice-cold 1× BC buffer and centrifuge at 974× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Flick the supernatant from the plate...
steps.instruction: Flick the supernatant from the plate and blot the plate on a clean paper towel to remove residual liquid.

steps.title: Resuspend each well in 50 μL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend each well in 50 μL of the appropriate barcode (refer to Table 3 ).

steps.title: Incubate the 96-well V-bottom plate on...
steps.instruction: Incubate the 96-well V-bottom plate on the thermomixer at 25 °C with 350 rpm shaking for 30 min.

steps.title: Add 140 μL of ice-cold 1×...
steps.instruction: Add 140 μL of ice-cold 1× BC buffer to all wells.

steps.title: Centrifuge the 96-well V-bottom at 974×...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the 96-well V-bottom at 974× g for 5 min at 4°C.

steps.title: Flick the supernatant from the plate...
steps.instruction: Flick the supernatant from the plate and blot on a clean paper towel to remove residual liquid.

steps.title: Resuspend the cells in 100 μL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cells in 100 μL of CSB and combine the appropriate wells (up to 15 barcoded samples should be combined into one tube) into an appropriately labeled 2 mL Eppendorf tube (BC Set 1–4, refer to Table 3 for labeling of 2 mL Eppendorf tubes for barcoded sets).

steps.title: Rinse each well with 100 μL...
steps.instruction: Rinse each well with 100 μL of CSB and collect the contents into a second set of 2 mL Eppendorf tubes labeled as wash tubes (BC 1–4 Wash, refer to Table 3 for labeling of wash tubes for each barcoded set).

steps.title: Centrifuge all tubes at 1,000× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge all tubes at 1,000× g in the microfuge for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Pool the primary and wash tubes...
steps.instruction: Pool the primary and wash tubes by resuspending each tube in 500 μL of CSB and transferring the contents of the wash tube to the primary tube.

steps.title: Add 800 μL of CSB to the primary tube
steps.instruction: Add 800 μL of CSB to the primary tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes at 1,000× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Measure the residual volume in each 2 mL Eppendorf
steps.instruction: Measure the residual volume in each 2 mL Eppendorf.

steps.title: If the volume is less than...
steps.instruction: If the volume is less than 50 μL per barcode sample, add enough CSB to ensure the final volume is equivalent to (# barcodes × 50 – residual volume) (i.e., 750 μL: residual volume for 15 barcodes). F. Surface staining and methanol permeabilization (2–3 h)

steps.title: Prepare the 2× surface master mix...
steps.instruction: Prepare the 2× surface master mix (refer to Table 4 ). Table

steps.title: Add 50 μL of 2× surface...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of 2× surface master mix per barcoded sample to each tube (15 pooled barcoded samples would use 750 μL of the surface master mix).

steps.title: Incubate the Eppendorf tubes on the...
steps.instruction: Incubate the Eppendorf tubes on the thermomixer at 25 °C with 350 rpm shaking for 30 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C and aspirate the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend in 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1.8 mL of CSB.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Repeat steps F5–7 once
steps.instruction: Repeat steps F5–7 once.

steps.title: Add 800 μL of ice-cold 100%...
steps.instruction: Add 800 μL of ice-cold 100% MeOH dropwise, followed by an additional 1 mL of 100% MeOH. Pause point: Samples can be stored at -80 °C overnight in 100% methanol to facilitate the workflow. If required, samples can be stored for 4–6 weeks in methanol. Note: If samples require intracellular staining, proceed to Section G. G. Intracellular staining and iridium intercalation (2–3 h)

steps.title: Remove the Eppendorf tubes from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the Eppendorf tubes from the -80 °C freezer and transfer them to wet ice for 30 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Resuspend in 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1.8 mL of CSB and centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Repeat steps G4–5 once
steps.instruction: Repeat steps G4–5 once.

steps.title: If the volume is less than...
steps.instruction: If the volume is less than 50 μL per barcode sample, add enough CSB to ensure the final volume is equivalent to (# barcodes × 50 – residual volume) (i.e., 750 μL: residual volume for 15 barcodes).

steps.title: Prepare the 2× intracellular master mix...
steps.instruction: Prepare the 2× intracellular master mix (refer to Table 5 ). Table

steps.title: Standard BioTools 3144015A K86-689
steps.instruction: Standard BioTools 3144015A K86-689.37 9 165 Ho pCREB (S

steps.title: Standard BioTools 3165034D 87G3 9 175 Lu pS6 (S235/S
steps.instruction: Standard BioTools 3165034D 87G3 9 175 Lu pS6 (S235/S

steps.title: Standard BioTools 3175009A N7-548 9 156...
steps.instruction: Standard BioTools 3175009A N7-548 9 156 Gd pP38 [T180/Y182] Standard BioTools 3156002A D3F9 18 171 Yb pERK 1/2 [T202/Y204] Standard BioTools 3171010A D13.14.4E 18 152 Sm pAkt (S

steps.title: Standard BioTools 3152005A D9E 36 153...
steps.instruction: Standard BioTools 3152005A D9E 36 153 Eu pSTAT1 [Y701] Standard BioTools 3153003A 58D6 36 158 Gd pSTAT3 [Y705] Standard BioTools 3158005A 4/P-STAT3 36 150 Nd pSTAT5 [Y694] Standard BioTools 3150005A 47 36 164 Dy IKβα Standard BioTools 3164004A L35A5 36 Volume of cell staining buffer (CSB) 3,357.00 The intracellular master mix is made in CSB to account for a 20% excess volume. For example, 60 samples require 3,600 μL of intracellular master mix. These antibody volumes have been used to stain up to 4 million cells.

steps.title: Add a volume of 2× intracellular...
steps.instruction: Add a volume of 2× intracellular master mix to each Eppendorf tube, equating to 50 μL per barcoded sample (15 pooled barcoded samples would require 750 μL of 2× intracellular mix).

steps.title: Incubate the Eppendorf tube in the...
steps.instruction: Incubate the Eppendorf tube in the thermomixer at 25 °C with 350 rpm shaking for 30 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C and aspirate the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend in 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1.8 mL of CSB, centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C, and aspirate supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat step G12 once
steps.instruction: Repeat step G12 once.

steps.title: Prepare enough volume of 1:2,000 iridium...
steps.instruction: Prepare enough volume of 1:2,000 iridium intercalation solution to account for 100 μL per barcoded sample.

steps.title: Resuspend cell pellets in freshly prepared iridium intercalation solution
steps.instruction: Resuspend cell pellets in freshly prepared iridium intercalation solution.

steps.title: Store at 4 °C for a minimum of 30 min
steps.instruction: Store at 4 °C for a minimum of 30 min. Pause point: Samples can be stored at 4 °C in iridium intercalation solution overnight. Do not exceed this duration. H. Sample preparation for Helios acquisition (6–10 h)

steps.title: Start up the Helios ~1 h
steps.instruction: Start up the Helios ~1 h: a. Start-up plasma (during this time, process samples as indicated below) ~20 min. b. Install nebulizer and check nebulizer spray ~2–5 min. c. Check background ~1–2 min. d. Tune ~16 min. e. Run EQ4 beads ~2 min. f. Run CAS + buffer for a minimum of 15 min to precondition the wide-bore injector.

steps.title: Retrieve samples from the 4 °C and mix by vortexing
steps.instruction: Retrieve samples from the 4 °C and mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Add enough CSB to each 2...
steps.instruction: Add enough CSB to each 2 mL Eppendorf tube to bring the total volume to 1.8 mL.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 1.8 mL of CSB.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Repeat steps H6–8 once
steps.instruction: Repeat steps H6–8 once.

steps.title: Pause point
steps.instruction: Pause point: Aspirate all but 50 μL of CSB from each tube. Notes:

steps.title: When you cannot run all samples...
steps.instruction: When you cannot run all samples on the same day, centrifuge and remove all but 50 μL of CSB from the 2 mL Eppendorf tube. Store samples at 4 °C, sealed with parafilm to prevent evaporation.

steps.title: These samples should be run as...
steps.instruction: These samples should be run as soon as possible, preferably the next day. Caution: Only samples that can be run in one day should be processed from CSB into CAS + to avoid cell loss.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 1.8 mL of CAS + .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 1.8 mL of CAS + .

steps.title: Repeat steps H11–13 once more
steps.instruction: Repeat steps H11–13 once more.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 1 mL of...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 1 mL of freshly prepared 1:10 EQ4 beads diluted in CAS + .

steps.title: Place the samples on ice
steps.instruction: Place the samples on ice.

steps.title: Remove 10 μL for counting using...
steps.instruction: Remove 10 μL for counting using a C-chip hemocytometer on the microscope.

steps.title: Adjust the concentration of cells to 1
steps.instruction: Adjust the concentration of cells to 1.3 × 10 6 cells/mL in 1:10 EQ4 beads diluted in CAS + . Aim to achieve an event rate of ~350–400 events/s (± 50 events/s). Note: The final volume for resuspension is dependent on the cell count from step H

steps.title: Proceed to run cells on the Helios mass cytometer
steps.instruction: Proceed to run cells on the Helios mass cytometer. Figure

steps.title: Representative data cleanup using Gaussian parameters for mass cytometry analysis
steps.instruction: Representative data cleanup using Gaussian parameters for mass cytometry analysis. Representative example of the data cleanup strategy utilizing Gaussian parameters, a critical step in the analysis pipeline for healthy whole blood samples. The presented data were derived from a single control donor and illustrate the expected outcome of this cleanup process, essential for ensuring data quality and reproducibility. Figure

steps.title: Surface immunophenotyping and relative frequencies of...
steps.instruction: Surface immunophenotyping and relative frequencies of immune cell subsets in healthy human whole blood. This figure details the strategy for identifying major immune cell subsets in healthy human whole blood using a 21-marker surface panel with CyTOF. To facilitate the analysis of cellular composition, whole blood samples were fixed, barcoded, and stained according to the established protocol. Single cells were defined after several cleanup steps (as detailed in Figure

steps.title: and the exclusion of doublets based...
steps.instruction: and the exclusion of doublets based on CD3 and CD20 expression. Subsequent gating was performed as follows: non-basophil leukocytes were identified as CD66b-CD45 + . T cells (CD3 + CD56-), NKT cells (CD3 + CD56 + ), and total NK cells (CD3-CD56 + ) were distinguished using CD3 and CD

steps.title: B cells were defined as CD123-CD3-CD56-CD11c-CD19 + CD20 +
steps.instruction: B cells were defined as CD123-CD3-CD56-CD11c-CD19 + CD20 + . Further subsetting of T and B cells was achieved using additional markers, including CD4, CD8, CD45RA, CD27, CD25, CD127, and IgD. Non-T and non-B cells (CD45 + CD3-CD19-CD56-CD20-) were gated as monocytes (CD14 +/- , CD11c + , CD16 +/- ), dendritic cells (HLA-DR + CD11c + ), and basophils (CD123 + ). (A) Bivariate plots illustrate the gating strategy used to identify major immune cell subsets in a representative healthy donor by CyTOF. (B) The stacked bar chart visualizes the relative frequency (% of total) of over 30 distinct cell populations defined by the expression of 21 surface markers from four (n =

steps.title: healthy donors
steps.instruction: healthy donors. Each stacked bar represents the average relative frequency of each immune subset of three biological replicates. This detailed immunophenotyping reveals the inherent variability in immune cell composition among healthy individuals. Figure

steps.title: T-cell subsetting and composition in healthy...
steps.instruction: T-cell subsetting and composition in healthy whole blood by mass cytometry. This figure illustrates the detailed gating strategy for identifying T-cell subsets in healthy human whole blood using a 21-surface marker immunophenotyping panel. Whole blood samples were processed (fixed, barcoded, and stained) as described in the protocol. Gaussian parameter cleanup and doublet exclusion (refer to Figure 2 for details) were performed before T-cell delineation. (A) Bivariate plots demonstrate the hierarchical gating strategy for T-cell subsets in a representative healthy donor. T cells were first identified as CD3 + CD56-. These were then subdivided into helper T cells (CD4 + ) and cytotoxic T cells (CD8 + ). Helper T cells were further classified into two categories: activated regulatory T cells (activated Tregs; CD127-CD25 + ) and CD4 + T effector (CD4 + Teff) cells. Both CD8 + and CD4 + T-effector cells were categorized based on their expression of CD27 and CD45RA into effector memory (TEM; CD27-CD45RA-), central memory (TCM; CD27 + CD45RA-), naive (Tnaive; CD27 + CD45RA + ), or terminally differentiated (TEMRA; CD27-CD45RA + ) subsets. (B) Bar graph depicting the relative frequency (%) of T-cell subsets in healthy donor whole blood (n = 4). Bars represent the average relative frequency of each subset, and error bars indicate the standard deviation (SD) across three technical replicates of the four healthy donors.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol was optimized using Jurkat cl E6-1 immortalized lymphoblastic leukemia cells and a smaller surface panel of antibodies, including CD45, CD3, and CD4, and the full panel of intracellular antibodies as described in Table 5 . The HIMC protocol was used to establish the phospho-response to stimulation with anti-CD3 (refer to Figure S2). Since endogenous T-cell activation requires both TCR engagement and co-stimulation, the SC activator cocktail, which is a proprietary reagent composed of both anti-CD3 and anti-CD28, was titrated and tested for its ability to recapitulate phospho-responses achieved with the HIMC protocol (refer to Figure S3). Results confirmed the use of the SC activator cocktail at a mid-dose of 50 µL/well and recapitulated the manufacturer’s suggestions. Using both T-cell stimuli, the kinetics of phosphorylation for the proteins involved in T-cell activation were assessed (Figure S4), and a 15-min stimulation length with the SC activator cocktail and the HIMC protocol was determined optimal for all further studies. Next, the optimized conditions were validated in healthy donor whole blood (n = 3) (refer to Table S1 and S2) using the surface and intracellular master mix panels described in Tables 4 and 5. An unstimulated control was included for each donor to normalize and calculate the fold change in median metal intensities for all intracellular cell state markers. Unstimulated samples were also used to establish the baseline phosphorylation status of the cell state proteins before stimulation (refer to Figure 5B and C for UNSTIM). IFNα and LPS stimuli were used as assay controls to confirm the success of the experiment (refer to Figure S5). Stimulation with IFNα elicits the phosphorylation of STAT1, STAT3, and STAT5 in the primary immune cell subsets, including T cells, B cells, natural killer (NK) cells, and monocytes (refer to Figure S6).
